_description: Wait Time Communications — status types, time thresholds, message templates

checked_in.label: Vehicle Checked In
checked_in.trigger: When vehicle is received at the shop
checked_in.message_goal: Confirm receipt and set initial time expectation

in_progress.label: Work In Progress
in_progress.message_goal: Reassure customer, provide estimate of completion time

needs_approval.label: Additional Work Found — Needs Approval
needs_approval.message_goal: Explain what was found, give price, ask for go/no-go
needs_approval.urgency: high — do not delay this message

parts_delay.label: Parts Delay
parts_delay.trigger: Ordered part will not arrive same day
parts_delay.message_goal: Honest update on delay, new ETA, offer loaner/transport info if available

ready_for_pickup.label: Vehicle Ready
ready_for_pickup.trigger: All work complete, final inspection passed
ready_for_pickup.message_goal: Clear pickup call to action, include total amount due and hours

extended_delay.label: Extended Delay (Same-Day Not Possible)
extended_delay.trigger: Job cannot be completed same day as promised
extended_delay.message_goal: Apologize, explain reason, give new completion date, offer transport

time_thresholds.note: If a job exceeds its category threshold with no update sent, trigger a proactive status message.

communication_rules: Never let more than 2 hours pass without a status update for waiting customers.
Always get verbal or text approval before performing any unplanned work.
If the customer is waiting in the lobby, communicate in person — skip the SMS.
Include the service advisor's name in messages when possible.
For 'needs_approval' messages, always include the dollar amount of the additional work.
loaner_and_transport_note: If shop offers loaner vehicles or has a ride-share arrangement, mention it in parts_delay and extended_delay messages.
